findings.message: Server certificates should be verified during SSL/TLS connections. Disabling certificate verification (e.g., curl -k, wget --no-check-certificate, pip --trusted-host) bypasses critical security checks and makes the connection vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks. Always verify server certificates or use proper certificate authorities.

Server certificates should be verified during SSL/TLS connections. Disabling certificate verification (e.g., curl -k, wget --no-check-certificate, pip --trusted-host) bypasses critical security checks and makes the connection vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks. Always verify server certificates or use proper certificate authorities.
